mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals an expansion in the supply chain, leading to higher demand for equities and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims signals a strengthening labor market, leading to higher equity prices and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 80% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts signals an expansion in the housing market, leading to higher demand for equities and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the GDP deflator signals an expansion in the supply chain, leading to higher demand for equities and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, leading to lower equity prices and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real disposable personal income signals an expansion in consumer demand, leading to higher equity prices and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the consumer price index signals an inflationary monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 80% of triggered instances.
